thread.title: Punjab byelections: AAP wins Dera Baba Nanak, Congress credits gangsters
thread.title_full: Punjab byelections: AAP wins Dera Baba Nanak, Congress credits gangsters

author: Yudhvir Rana
title: Punjab byelections: AAP wins Dera Baba Nanak, Congress credits gangsters
text: GURDASPUR: Gurdeep Singh Randhawa of the Aam Aadmi Party won the Dera Baba Nanak assembly byelection by 5,699 votes on Saturday, leaving Congress candidate Jatinder Kaur Randhawa to the second spot and Bharatiya Janata Party’s Ravi Karan Singh Kahlon to a distant third with only 6,505 votes. The Congress has accused AAP of using gangsters for this victory.Held on Nov 13, this byelection was necessitated after Jatinder Kaur’s husband, Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa, vacated the seat after being elected to Parliament. Gurdeep Singh Randhawa secured 59,104 votes, while Jatinder Kaur Randhawa garnered 53,405.In the 2022 Punjab assembly elections, Gurdeep Singh Randhawa had received 31,742 mandates, while the Congress had polled 52,555. The counting of votes, conducted in 18 rounds at the Sukhjinder Group of Institutes in Gurdaspur, began with postal ballots. By the 10th round, Randhawa overtook Jatinder Kaur in the vote tally and maintained his lead until the end.In his first statement following the win, Gurdeep Randhawa said: “This is a victory over nepotism and arrogance, which I have been highlighting consistently throughout my campaign. Today, the voters have affirmed this message.”Displaying his trademark confidence, Randhawa said while twirling his moustache: “It victory, achieved by the grace of God, is not mine but of every worker who stood by me.”Jatinder Kaur, who has lost her maiden electoral battle, was unavailable for comment, but her husband, Congress MP Sukhjinder Singh Randhawa attributed the loss to two factors — influence of alleged “gangster elements” and the support that the Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) had extended to AAP after pulling out of the race.He claimed: “The Congress didn’t lose its voter base, but the AAP won this election with the help of gangsters.” He remains optimistic about the party’s prospects in 2027’s Punjab assembly elections.Sukhjinder Randhawa said: “The Congress received more votes than it had in 2022, and if that wasn’t enough to secure a win, this indicates that the party’s traditional vo- ter base didn’t expand or shrink. It doesn’t point to a shift in voter sentiment. The outside support to AAP implies that Gurdeep Randhawa’s campaign was mobilised more effectively and that he also benefited possibly from the new alliances.”
